Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Per Ex Parte Order Filed 2/28/20
Off Calendar Mar-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Wensheng Ma, esq. (no number provided) for Plaintiff via court call.
Nicole Healy, esq. (650-364-1609) and Edwin Barnes, esq. of Ropers...Bentley for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction Per Ex Parte Order Filed 2/28/20

Defendants Greg Rudin, Mason Blake, Matthew Faustman and UpCounsel, Inc.'s OSC re preliminary injunction is granted. Raj Abhyanker, Trustee of the Raj and Sonal Abhyanker Family Trust, and counsel for plaintiffs through his law firm, LegalForce RABC, and his or its trustees, agents, employees and those persons or entities in active concert or participation with any of them are restrained and enjoined from disclosing on your personal internet blog or by any other means to any third-party the confidential documents or confidential information pertaining to the LinkedIn transaction (e.g. Waiver, Release, and License Agreement and term sheet).

Injunctive relief is appropriate to restrain the disclosure of confidential information. (See Empire Steam Laundry v. Lozier (1913) 165 Cal. 95, 100 [it is "settled beyond peradventure" that "equity will always protect against the unwarranted disclosure of trade secrets and confidential communications and the like"]; Courtesy Temporary Service, Inc. v. Camacho (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1278, 1290-1291 [former employee's use of employer's confidential information was enjoinable].)

Defendants demonstrate that plaintiff is likely to breach the parties' confidentiality agreement and/or breach the confidentiality requirements of mediation. (See Evid. Code 1119(c) and 1126.) The parties agree that they entered into a confidentiality agreement. (Barnes Affidavit, pars. 5-6, 13; Mason Affidavit, par. 10; Abhyanker Dec., par. 7.) The parties exchanged confidential information pursuant to that agreement and as part of a mediation and settlement efforts. (Barnes Affidavit, par. 4-7.) Defendants demonstrate irreparable harm because plaintiffs have indicated that they are planning to disclose the confidential information. Although defendant Abhyanker contends that no injunction is warranted because "he already agreed not to publish any of the disputed information or documents without consent until after the anticipated litigation concludes" (Opp. at 3: 10-11), in virtually the same breath he retracts that assertion and instead asserts the right and entitlement "to publish now." (Abhyanker Dec., pars. 16, 17.) Plaintiffs fail to show that injunctive relief is not warranted because defendants purportedly materially breached the confidentiality agreement by submitting the Blake and Faustman declarations in support of their motion to dismiss that described LegalForce Worldwide RAPC and related persons' ownership interest in the UpCounsel stock. Plaintiffs never sought to seal any of the information in those declarations, nor do two wrongs make a right. Based upon defendants' likelihood of prevailing on the merits and the balance of equities and harms, the court grants the injunction.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
